author: y4rr
content: Resolution: for shuffled+drop_last configs, the comparison cannot be SET-equality against torch's kept-items (different PRNGs drop different items). Instead, verify the semantic invariant: rust's output items form a valid subset of the original 10-item dataset, all unique, of expected length. The pin script's meta.json carries the FULL 10-item ground truth so the verifier can check (a) each rust item exists in the reference dataset (b) no duplicates (c) count == expected (B*num_batches for drop_last=True). This validates 'rust DataLoader respects drop_last semantics + shuffles a permutation' without requiring PRNG match.
